acceptance.assertion: The repository documents and implements a visual contract derived from official Miro object, diagram, presentation and large-board guidance.
Board specifications select frames, shapes, connectors, tables, documents and sticky notes by information function and reject misuse.
Quality v2 blocks missing narrative structure, weak hierarchy, excessive density, decorative colour use, connector clutter and unseparated evidence even when raw object counts look healthy.
A deterministic renderer produces a compact seven-frame reference board with a clear reading path, consistent grid and semantic visual language.
A new allowlisted Education-team board is created through Schauwerk, remotely read back and visually reviewed without altering or deleting existing boards.
All repository tests and CI pass and the merged diff has a head- and diff-bound critical self-review.

approval.note: The user explicitly authorized plan, registration, repository implementation and one new bounded live Miro reference board in one run.

goal: Implement the complete Visual System v2 vertical slice in Schauwerk and prove it with a newly created, visually reviewed Miro Education reference board.

does_not_establish: automatic aesthetic acceptance from a score

post_merge_validation.result: 536 tests plus Ruff, compileall and registry validation passed

metadata.outcome: Visual System v2 is merged and live-proved with semantic object choice, fail-closed design gates, exact Miro conformance and a separate visual review.
metadata.source_request: User rejected the visual quality of the first Miro practice board and authorized a full redesign without preserving prior visual preferences.

title: Implement and live-prove Visual System v2
